'''R4-K5''' was an [[R-series/Legends|R-series]] [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] that accompanied [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], formerly [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Anakin Skywalker, on missions for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] early during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. Once owned by a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], R4-K5 was chosen for its dark [[Color/Legends|hue]] by Vader to replace his former astromech [[Droid/Legends|droid]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]].

R4-K5 occupied the [[Astromech socket/Legends|droid socket]] of Vader's [[Darth Vader's black Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|customized]] [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2 interceptor]] during missions to the [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of [[Murkhana/Legends|Murkhana]] and [[Ostor]]. While competent at its duties, the droid never shared a close relationship with its [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] master as R2-D2 once had with Skywalker; R4-K5 was treated by Vader as simple equipment.

==Biography==
An [[R-series/Legends|R-series]] [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], R4-K5 was once owned by a [[Jedi/Legends|member]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].<ref name="HC" /> In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], recently minted as the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, [[Duel on Mustafar/Legends|sustained]] extensive injuries at the hands of his former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]].<ref name="EPIII">{{Film|III}}</ref> Forced to don a [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|black suit of armor]], Vader also acquired a [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Darth Vader's black Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2 ''Actis''-class interceptor]] in the first [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. R4-K5 was chosen by Vader as his new astromech [[Droid/Legends|droid]], replacing Skywalker's astromech [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], as the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] saw its drab shell as a reflection of his own [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|darkness]].<ref name="HC" />

[[File:VaderR4K5-DVATCOS4.jpg|thumb|left|200px|R4-K5 aboard Vader's interceptor as Vader leads Green Squadron above Ostor]]
R4-K5 was stationed on the new [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Exactor/Legends|''Exactor'']], in the [[Starship/Legends|vessel's]] number three [[Hangar/Legends|launching bay]], along with Vader's [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|interceptor]]. Over two [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after the [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the droid occupied the [[Astromech socket/Legends|astromech socket]] of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord's]] [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptor]] during an [[Mission to Murkhana (Great Jedi Purge)|attack]] on fugitive [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Roan Shryne]] and [[Olee Starstone]], as the pair made an escape from the formerly [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]]-aligned Outer Rim world of [[Murkhana/Legends|Murkhana]]. R4-K5 was instrumental in assisting Vader in grasping the tactical situation, supplying him with information regarding the escape plan of the [[CloakShape fighter/Legends|CloakShape fighter]] being used by the Jedi, as well as alerting him to their imminent absconsion by way of a [[Hyperspace transport ring/Legends|hyperdrive ring]]. Despite the droid's assistance, Vader was unable to destroy his targets before they outran his interceptor.<ref name="DL">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref>

[[Standard month/Legends|Months]] later, R4-K5 flew with Vader as part of [[Green Squadron (Galactic Empire)|Green Squadron]] during the second phase of the [[Shrouded Offensive]], an extensive and ultimately ill-fated campaign against a [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdout]] on the planet [[Ostor]]. Vader, on orders from [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to launch a direct airborne strike after the failure of an initial [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground assault]], led the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] in an attack on a [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielded]] [[City/Legends|city]], and engaged in a [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]] with [[V-19 Torrent starfighter/Legends|V-19 Torrent fighters]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by followers of rogue [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[Kaddak (clone trooper)|Kaddak]]. During the combat, Imperial [[Clone trooper pilot/Legends|clone pilot]] [[Hock Malsuum]] realized that the squadron had left the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] from which they had launched vulnerable, and so Vader and R4-K5 made an about-face to defend it. The battle ultimately ended in a pyrrhic victory for the Empire.<ref name="COS">[[Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows 4|''Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows'' 4]]</ref>

==Characteristics==
[[File:R4K5-TSC066.png|thumb|right|180px|R4-K5, Imperial astromech]]
R4-K5 featured a round silver dome with tool panels and a [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]], both of which were black, and the dome also possessed a red status indicator. The droid's plating was dark-gray with black tool coverings.<ref name="DB" /> A [[Class two droid/Legends|class two droid]]<ref name="TNEGTD">According to ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]] are classified as [[Class two droid/Legends|class two droid]]s.</ref> manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]], R4-K5 stood at a height of 0.96 [[Meter/Legends|meters]].<ref name="DB" /> Additionally, R4-K5 was able to interface with the systems of the Eta-2 ''Actis''-class interceptor and analyze possible targets such as [[Skiff/Legends|skiffs]] and [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] rings, transferring this data to the interceptor's [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] displays upon the pilot's request.<ref name="DL" /> R4-K5 did not share the close bond Skywalker had maintained with his former astromech, R2-D2; rather, the droid was simply regarded by Vader as another piece of machinery.<ref name="DB" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Notable appearances===
{{Quote|This was not the craft Anakin Skywalker had piloted to Mustafar, and the starfighter's socketed astromech droid had a black dome.|An excerpt from ''Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader'', featuring R4-K5's first appearance|<ref name="DL" />}}
[[File:Arfourkayfivehasbro.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The R4-K5 action figure]]
R4-K5 made its first appearance in the novel ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'', which was written by [[James Luceno]] and released in [[2005]]. Not referred to by designation, R4-K5 is initially described as an astromech with a black dome, and is mentioned twice more as "the astromech."<ref name="DL" /> The R4-K5 designation was provided by [[Hasbro]] in [[2006]] alongside an action figure based on the droid.<ref name="HC" />

The droid later appeared briefly in the [[2008]] [[Canon|non-canon]] ''[[Soulcalibur IV]]'' tie-in [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] ''[[Star Wars: Visions of the Blade]]'', in which R4-K5 makes an appearance in one panel socketed aboard Vader's interceptor. The droid accompanies the Sith Lord to a [[Unidentified planet (Cowl Crucible)|planet]] in the [[Cowl Crucible]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]], where Vader [[Battle of the Cowl Crucible|encounters]] ''Soulcalibur'' character [[Mitsurugi]].<ref name="VOTB">''[[Star Wars: Visions of the Blade]]''</ref> In the comic arc ''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Lost Command]]'', Vader's now heavily modified interceptor is equipped with an astromech droid; however, it is depicted inconsistently between frames and is not confirmed to be R4-K5.<ref name="DVLC">''[[Star Wars: Darth Vader and the Lost Command]]''</ref>

===Inconsistencies===
R4-K5 is presented inconsistently in terms of color across sources. In the 2005 reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', in which the droid was first pictured, R4-K5 is seen as having a black dome, with a white body.<ref name="TNEC" /> Later, several Hasbro figures of the droid portray R4-K5 with a dark-gray body, silver dome, and black panels,<ref name="HC" /><ref name="Transformers">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars Transformers|pack=Darth Vader – Sith Starfighter|altlink=https://toys.tfw2005.com/darth-vader-sith-starfighter-1407}}</ref> or alternatively with a purple body.<ref name="HC1">{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: 30th Anniversary Collection|pack=Darth Vader's Sith Starfighter|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/tacsithstarfighter.asp}}</ref> In the comic [[Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows 4|''Darth Vader and the Cry of Shadows'' 4]], R4-K5 is shown with a black body with silver tool panels.<ref name="COS" /> This article treats the version of R4-K5 depicted in the [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] as the correct one.<ref name="DB" />
